step_by_step_thinking:
Dentin conditioning typically involves applying a substance to alter certain characteristics of the dentin prior to bonding agents or restoratives. From the given articles, one key reference discusses how "[p]olyacrylic acid...applied for 10 seconds to increase the surface energy and wettability of the tooth." This supports that dentin conditioning increases surface energy to improve wetting and thus enhances adhesive interactions.
Therefore, my answer is \boxed{B}. Based on the evidence showing polyacrylic acid increasing surface energy and wettability of dentin for better chemical bonding, the correct answer is \boxed{B} - Increases surface energy of dentin.
